item.title: Malaysia rolls out red carpet for PM Tarique
item.description: A red carpet was rolled out as prime minister Tarique Rahman arrived in Kuala Lumpur on Sunday on an official visit at the invitation of his Malaysian counterpart Anwar Ibrahim, beginning his first overseas tour......

item.title: Flawed policy allows vehicles to honk far above WHO limits
item.description: Bangladesh permits vehicle horn volumes that are globally regarded harmful to human health, raising serious public health concerns......

item.title: Banks’ dollar holdings keep falling
item.description: Commercial banks’ foreign currency holdings declined in May from a year earlier despite record remittance inflows and improved stability in the foreign exchange market, as Bangladesh Bank continued to absorb dollars from banks while import payments remained high......

item.title: Govt weighs alternative funding source
item.description: Finance and planning minister Amir Khosru Mahmud Chowdhury on Sunday said they had to look for alternative funding sources rather than relying on loans from multilateral lenders......

item.title: Islami Bank Grahak Forum stages sit-in
item.description: The Islami Bank Sachetan Grahak Forum on Sunday staged a sit-in programme in front of the head office of Islami Bank Bangladesh PLC to press for its seven-point demand aimed at stabilising the bank and restoring corporate governance......

item.title: BSEC, DSE vow to combat market manipulation
item.description: The Bangladesh Securities and Exchange Commission and the Dhaka Stock Exchange on Sunday reaffirmed their commitment to preventing market manipulation and strengthening investor protection through enhanced surveillance and regulatory oversight......

item.title: IMCB seeks 60pc quota for consultants
item.description: The Institute of Management Consultants Bangladesh on Monday urged the government to increase the mandatory participation of local consultants to 60 per cent from the existing 30 per cent in development projects where Bangladesh has proven expertise......

item.title: Housing may face crisis from July 1 over proposed flat tax: REHAB
item.description: The Real Estate and Housing Association of Bangladesh on Sunday warned that the housing sector could face a major setback from July 1 if the proposed 15 per cent tax on flats received by landowners under joint development agreements took effect, citing risks to investment, housing affordability and urban development......

item.title: Govt boosting online one-stop investment services: Muktadir
item.description: Commerce minister Khandakar Abdul Muktadir on Sunday said that the government is strengthening efforts to introduce a centralized online one-stop investment service to create a more business-friendly environment by simplifying and integrating licensing and approval procedures......

item.title: Sonali Bank launches live verification for pensioners
item.description: Sonali Bank PLC on Sunday started biometric registration and live verification for pensioners through biometric system intermigration with Sonali e-Sheba app, said a press release......

item.title: CAAB launches national civil aviation master plan
item.description: Bangladesh on Sunday formally launched the process of formulating its first-ever integrated ‘Civil Aviation Master Plan’ to guide the sustainable and long-term development of the country’s aviation sector over the next three decades......

item.title: Stocks slump for profit-taking snaps
item.description: Stocks retreated on Sunday, ending a two-session gaining streak as investors booked profits in recently appreciated shares, outweighing selective buying in fundamentally strong stocks......

item.title: Pakistan mango exports shrink as ME war impacts linger
item.description: Beneath the scorching sun in Pakistan’s southern mango belt, labourers balance on tree branches, working at a swift pace to throw the freshly picked fruit into sacks held ready by farmhands waiting below......

item.title: Are the EU and China heading for a trade war?
item.description: The European Union’s trade deficit with China is growing, prompting Brussels to take protective measures against everything from electric vehicles to e-commerce platforms and deepening fears of a trade war......

item.title: Europe risks ‘total irrelevance’ without sovereign tech: Cohere
item.description: Europe risks ‘total technological irrelevance’ if it fails to develop sovereign capabilities, the head of Canadian AI startup Cohere warned Thursday on the sidelines of the Vivatech trade fair in Paris......

item.title: Robots pour cocktails, run marathons, but still can’t multitask
item.description: They can mix cocktails, run marathons and fold laundry. But humanoid robots are still a long way from doing lots of different jobs on command, whatever the marketing says......

item.title: 89 campaigns win Digital Marketing Award
item.description: At the 9th Digital Marketing Award, an initiative of Bangladesh Brand Forum, presented by Meghna Group of Industries and powered by Walton Refrigerator, 89 campaigns across 26 distinct categories were recognised......

item.title: BRAC receives Tk 110 crore in dividends from BRAC Bank
item.description: BRAC Bank and BRAC marked the distribution of BRAC’s share of the dividend declared by the bank for the 2025 financial year at an event titled ‘BRAC Family – For the People, For the Country’ held on June 18 in the capital......

item.title: Dhaka mustn’t agree UN calls that harm Rohingya return
item.description: THE UN refugee agency has once again put out a call for Bangladesh to arrange for more land for the construction of shelters for the 150,000 Rohingyas who have freshly entered Bangladesh from Myanmar between November 2023 and April 27, 2025. The Office of the Refugee Relief and Repatriation Committee has said that the Office of the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees has......

item.title: Exclusion of women weakens democracy
item.description: WOMEN’S political participation, particularly in parliamentary politics, remains a far cry because of gender-based harassment, societal bias and institutional barriers, as a recent BRAC Institute of Governance Studies report says. The report was based on a study based on 43 in-depth interviews with women parliamentarians, among others, alongside a review of relevant......

item.title: Who will defend the poor?
item.description: THE post-July uprising landscape in Bangladesh perfectly illustrates the prevailing sentiment that without structural change in governance, the transformative aspirations for which more than a thousand people laid down their lives in July 2024 will never be realised. After fifteen years of tyranny under the Hasina regime, this February’s election can be marked merely as......

item.title: Credentialism in employment market
item.description: BANGLADESH has seen remarkable growth in education, higher education institutions and professional certification programmes. While this expansion has contributed to human capital development, it has also given rise to a phenomenon commonly referred to as ‘credential culture,’ in which academic degrees, certificates and formal qualifications are often valued more......

item.title: Food security, climate resilience, social justice
item.description: HOUSING challenge is no longer merely a question of how many houses exist. It is increasingly a question of whether the houses are safe, healthy, climate-resilient and capable of supporting human dignity. While national development discussions often focus on economic growth, infrastructure, energy, education and healthcare, housing remains one of the most......

item.title: Missing research investment
item.description: BANGLADESH often articulates an ambition to become a knowledge-based economy, a hub of innovation and a confident actor in the digital age. Policy documents, ministerial speeches and university-level strategic plans regularly invoke the language of research, technological advancement and evidence-based policymaking. Yet beneath this aspirational rhetoric lies......

item.title: Brazil must adapt to loss of injured Raphinha: Paqueta
item.description:
Brazil must find a way to overcome the loss of injured star Raphinha as quickly as possible, midfielder Lucas Paqueta said Sunday, as Carlo Ancelotti's side prepare for their final group game of the World Cup against Scotland.
...

item.title: 'Absurd' to doubt Spain, says De la Fuente after Saudi Arabia rout
item.description: Spain boss Luis de la Fuente said his side had silenced their doubters after Lamine Yamal and Mikel Oyarzabal inspired a 4-0 thrashing of Saudi Arabia on Sunday....

item.title: No pressure to pass to Portugal star Ronaldo: Conceicao
item.description: Portugal winger Francisco Conceicao insisted on Sunday his team-mates felt no pressure to pass to Cristiano Ronaldo following scrutiny of the veteran superstar's role in their lacklustre World Cup opener....

item.title: Belgium held by Iran as Ngoy sent off
item.description: Belgium were held to a scoreless draw by Iran in a frustrating encounter Sunday that saw the Red Devils reduced to 10 men and facing the possibility of group-stage elimination for a second straight World Cup....

item.title: Enforcement of environmental laws urged to curb pollution
item.description: Environmental activists, government officials and stakeholders at a discussion on Sunday called for strict enforcement of environmental laws to combat growing levels of air, water, soil and noise pollution across the country......

item.title: 5 sentenced to death for Bogura child murder
item.description: A Bogura court on Sunday sentenced five people to death and five others to 10 years’ imprisonment for the murder of eight-year-old Rifat Hossain at Shahjahanpur in the district in 2018......

item.title: Govt to recruit 1 lakh health workers, 25,000 midwives
item.description: Prime minister’s special assistant on health affairs SM Ziauddin Hyder on Sunday said that the government planned to recruit 1,00,000 new health workers and 25,000 midwives over the next three to four years to ensure primary healthcare services of the people......

item.title: Imam sent to jail over rape of 5-year-old girl in Mymensingh
item.description: A Mymensingh court on Sunday sent an imam to jail in connection with a case filed over the rape of a five-year-old girl at Trishal upazila......

item.title: Literacy, numeracy govt priorities in pry education: Bobby Hajjaj
item.description: In primary education the government prioritised foundational literacy and numeracy, state minister for primary and mass education Bobby Hajjaj said on Sunday......

item.title: Entrepreneur Selima Ahmad dies
item.description: Bangladesh Women Chamber of Commerce and Industry president and former director of Federation of Bangladesh Chambers of Commerce and Industry Selima Ahmad passed away while undergoing treatment at a hospital in Thailand on Sunday......

item.title: Sylhet tourist spot shut, 3,000 families stranded in Lalmonirhat as rivers swell
item.description: A popular tourist spot, Sadapathar in Sylhet, was shut down on Sunday amid continued swelling of rivers and onrush of water from upstream following heavy rainfall in Bangladesh and its adjacent parts of India......

item.title: Lightning kills 3 students in Narsingdi
item.description: Three madrasa students were killed in a lightning strike while performing ablution in Narsingdi’s Monohardi......

item.title: Thakurgaon potato farmers seek lower cold storage fees
item.description: Potato farmers and traders in Thakurgaon on Sunday formed a human chain and submitted a memorandum to the deputy commissioner demanding that cold storage charges be reduced to Tk 5 per kilogram......

item.title: Minister calls for science-based, coordinated anti-drug efforts to protect youth
item.description: Social welfare and women and children affairs minister AZM Zahid Hossain at a seminar in Dhaka on Sunday called for taking international standard science-based and integrated approaches to protect the youth from the curses of drug abuse......

item.title: Man kills brother inside mosque during prayers in Barishal
item.description: A man allegedly hacked his younger brother to death inside a mosque during prayers over a long-standing land dispute at Mehendiganj in Barishal on Sunday......

item.title: Shibir leader killed over school managing body row
item.description: A local leader of the Bangladesh Islami Chhatrashibir was killed and another man seriously injured in an alleged attack over a dispute surrounding the election of a government primary school’s managing committee president at Saghata in Gaibandha on Sunday......

item.title: SMUCT hosts discussion on employment challenges, RMG industry future
item.description: A discussion titled Employment Challenges and the Future of Industry was held at the Shanto-Mariam University of Creative Technology in Dhaka on Sunday......

item.title: Navy chief urges new officers to be ready for ultimate sacrifice for sovereignty
item.description: Chief of the Bangladesh Navy Admiral M Nazmul Hassan in Chattogram on Sunday urged the newly commissioned officers to remain imbued with honesty, dedication and self-sacrifice and be ready to make the ultimate sacrifice for safeguarding the country’s independence and sovereignty......

item.title: World Music Day celebrated
item.description: Cultural organisations celebrated World Music Day 2026 with musical processions and music shows across the capital on Sunday......

item.title: Environment minister calls for working together to control noise pollution
item.description: Environment, forest and climate change minister Abdul Awal Mintu at a workshop in Dhaka on Sunday called upon everyone to work collectively to control air and noise pollution......

item.title: Low funding threatens renewable energy goal
item.description: Civil society bodies warned that 98 per cent of the energy budget remained locked into fossil fuels despite policy commitments, adding that Bangladesh required at least Tk 21,750 crore in annual investment to achieve its renewable energy targets by 2030......

item.title: Challenges facing plainsland nat’l minority communities highlighted
item.description: A seminar titled ‘Contemporary Issues, Challenges and the Way Forward for Plain Land National Minority Peoples’ was held at the CBCB Centre in Mohammadpur, Dhaka, on Saturday, according to a press release......

item.title: Govt announces ‘Nazrul Barsha’
item.description: The government on Sunday officially announced ‘Nazrul Barsha’ to mark the 127th birth anniversary of national poet Kazi Nazrul Islam......

item.title: CWASA to supply water to CUET
item.description: The long-standing water crisis at Chittagong University of Engineering and Technology is going to be resolved in response to the application of the Association of Engineers Bangladesh......

item.title: ULAB launches creative writing certificate course
item.description: The University of Liberal Arts Bangladesh has launched the creative writing certificate course 2026, a six-week intensive programme on writing fiction......

item.title: Iranians walk out of talks venue after Trump threat
item.description: ‘The Iranian delegation remains engaged in the talks and has not indicated to the mediators any intention to leave,’ the diplomat said on condition of anonymity......

item.title: DU holds workshop on foot pressure scan
item.description: The biomedical physics and technology department of Dhaka University and the BYBIT Limited jointly held a workshop titled Foot Pressure Scan to Prevent Diabetic Amputation at the Curzon Hall of DU on Sunday......

item.title: HC questions dismissing harassment cases
item.description: The High Court on Sunday asked the government to explain why a provision of the Cyber Safety Act, 2026 mandating the blanket dismissal of pending cases under several provisions of the repealed Digital Security Act should not be declared unlawful......

item.title: DMP to deploy 18,000 cops on AL founding anniversary
item.description: The Dhaka Metropolitan Police on Sunday night announced extensive security measures across the capital ahead of the founding anniversary of Awami League,......

item.title: Ukrainian strikes on Crimea kill 4, pause fuel sales
item.description: Four people were killed and fuel sales were suspended in Russian-occupied Crimea, the Moscow-backed authorities there said on Sunday, after a massive Ukrainian barrage hit the Black Sea peninsula......

item.title: Falling fertiliser stocks may hurt monsoon crops
item.description: Cultivation of monsoon crops, including Aus, Aman and jute, is now facing risk of disruption due to declining stock of major fertilisers amid low domestic productions......

item.title: Timeless Messi eyes World Cup record as Argentina face Austria
item.description:
The Lionel Messi show rolls into Dallas with the Argentine maestro on the cusp of more footballing history when the World Cup holders face Austria on Monday.
...

item.title: Argentina face Austria with knockout stage in sight
item.description: Defending champions Argentina can take a major step ​towards the knockout stage when they face Austria in their second World Cup Group J match on Monday, ‌after both sides opened their campaigns with convincing victories......

item.title: Israelis believe Iran won Mideast war: poll
item.description: Israelis overwhelmingly believe that Iran emerged stronger from the Middle East war and its subsequent deal with the United States, a poll released on Sunday found......

item.title: After failed push-in bid, five still stranded in no-man’s land
item.description: Five people still remained stranded in the no-man’s-land along the Kurigram border at Rahumari upazila for over the past seven days, after a push-in attempt by the Indian Border Security Force was foiled by the Border Guard Bangladesh members and local residents......

item.title: Japan striker Ueda channels frustration to send World Cup warning
item.description: Ayase Ueda warned Sweden his confidence was sky-high after the red-hot striker scored a classy World Cup brace to put Japan on the cusp of the knockout rounds....

item.title: BNP MP’s son in DB custody for questioning

item.title: Yamal scores on injury return as Spain thrash Saudi Arabia
item.description: Lamine Yamal's return sparked Spain's World Cup into life as the European champions ran riot to beat Saudi Arabia 4-0 in Atlanta....

item.title: Vance hopeful of new chapter in US-Iran talks
item.description: US vice-president JD Vance expressed hopes on Sunday of turning a ‘new leaf’ during talks with Iran in Switzerland, even as the threat of renewed fighting in Lebanon and lack of consensus on key issues rendered prospects for a permanent deal elusive......

item.title: AL leader dies, 11 others held over flash processions
item.description: An Awami League leader died while reportedly trying to evade arrest, one was arrested, and 10 other activists of the party, activities of which are now banned, were detained on Sunday from flash processions staged in several areas across the country......
